title: Walton-on-the-Naze VII Memorial
crew_summary: 8 killed including Flt Lt. John George Clothier

external_narratives.summary: Imperial War Museums register entry for the memorial to the crew of Halifax Bomber RG475, 432 Squadron RCAF, at Walton-on-the-Naze. The crew were lost on the night of 5/6 March 1945 returning from an operational mission.
external_narratives.key_details: Crew: S/L Edwin Alfred Hayes (pilot), Flt Lt John George Clothier, P/O Douglas Marvin Cooke, F/O Colin Maxwell Hay, P/O D. Ringrose, Flt Lt Glenn Royal Harris, P/O Marius Bendt Nielsen, P/O Gilbert Melbourne Orser. Seven buried at Brookwood Cemetery, Surrey; one at Monmouth.

external_narratives.summary: Registry entry for the Halifax RG475 memorial monument at Walton-on-the-Naze. Documents the 432 (Leaside) Squadron RCAF loss and the memorial erected in 1979.
external_narratives.key_details: Halifax VII designated QO-L with 8 recorded operations. Airscrew blades were recovered in 1979 by officers and cadets of the Colchester and Clacton Air Training Corps. Memorial dedicated that same year.

external_narratives.summary: Official RCAF Association heritage page for 432 (Leaside) Squadron, documenting the unit's wartime history operating from East Moor as part of 6 Group (RCAF) Bomber Command.
external_narratives.key_details: 432 Squadron was part of the all-Canadian 6 Group, Bomber Command. Operated Halifax aircraft from RAF East Moor, Yorkshire. The squadron suffered significant losses in the final months of the war.
external_narratives.notes: URL dead as of 2026-04-06; URL dead as of 2026-04-06

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

sources: Current 51.894 0.445 ~57km west — longitude error (should be +1.27 not +0.45)
location_confidence.verification_notes: CORRECTION. Current longitude (0.445) ~57km west of Walton-on-the-Naze. Coastal town CO14 at 51.848, +1.273.
